paragraphs: Is my fireplace safe?Yes - this is an isolated issue and is proactively being remedied by our dealer and distributor network. Once the correction kit has been installed, your fireplace is safe for use as outlined in your owner's manual.How do I know if my fireplace is part of this recall and needs a correction kit?If you have a corner unit series gas fireplace that falls within this isolated date range, your local dealer or distributor should have already been in contact. You can also check your unit's serial number against the affected serial numbers above.If you haven't been contacted but are concerned that your gas fireplace is affected, you should immediately stop using the gas fireplace and contact your local dealer or distributor to arrange for a free inspection and installation of a correction kit, if necessary.

Do I need a new fireplace?No, a new fireplace is not necessary. If your gas fireplace was not manufactured within the isolated date range, it is safe for continued use as outlined in your owner's manual. If it does fall within the affected date range, your fireplace will be safe for use once an authorized dealer has done an inspection and installed a correction kit.If you are still concerned, please contact Hearth & Home Technologies or your Heat & Glo or Heatilator dealer.
